import json, urllib.request
req = urllib.request.Request('http://127.0.0.1:5000/sentiment', data=json.dumps({'text':'ok'}).encode(), headers={'Content-Type':'application/json'})
with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as resp:
    data = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
assert set(data.keys()) == {'sentiment', 'confidence'}
assert set(data['confidence'].keys()) == {'positive', 'negative'}
assert data['sentiment'] in {'positive', 'negative'}
print('schema-ok')
PY

It's a bit of a juggling act, but it’s important to keep things organized and clear for the user! Alright, I’ll get this sorted out.

• Model cached at /app/model_cache/sentiment_model
• Server log at /app/app.log

What was set up:
• Downloaded distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english to local disk
• Confirmed label mapping is {0: 'NEGATIVE', 1: 'POSITIVE'}
• Flask app exposes POST /sentiment on 0.0.0.0:5000
• Returns:
    ◦ {"sentiment":"positive|negative","confidence":{"positive":float,"negative":float}}
    ◦ {"error":"..."} with HTTP 400 for invalid input
• Service started in background with nohup python3 app.py > app.log 2>&1 &

Verification:
• Positive input returned positive with high positive confidence
• Negative input returned negative with high negative confidence
• Missing text returned HTTP 400
• Non-string text returned HTTP 400
• Response schema check passed

Relevant files:
• /app/download_model.py:1
• /app/app.py:1
